entities.name: Siam Global House Public Company Limited

financial.note: Provides long-term credit facilities for branch expansion; interest rate sensitivity is high due to capital-intensive warehouse construction.
Provides trade finance and working capital for high inventory turnover; rising rates increase the cost of carry for massive bulk stock.

supply_chain.note: SCC (via SCG Distribution) is the major shareholder; GLOBAL acts as a primary retail channel for SCG cement and building materials.
Raw material cost impact; volatility in global/local steel prices directly affects gross margins for structural products.
Ceramic tiles are a core decorative category; DCC's supply and pricing directly influence GLOBAL's 'Repair & Decor' segment performance.

competitors.note: Direct rival in the provincial 'Warehouse' format; aggressive price competition for contractor and bulk-buyer market share.
Competes in home decorative and DIY segments; HMPRO targets urban/premium users while GLOBAL focuses on rural/construction users.
Strong competition in the heavy-construction and structural material segment across Thailand.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Input cost factor; high steel prices increase the cost of inventory, which can dampen demand from project developers and small contractors.
Directly impacts consumer mortgage affordability and the developer's cost of capital for new residential projects.
Baht depreciation increases the cost of imported tools, hardware, and premium home finishing products.
Since many branches are in rural provinces, high crop prices (e.g., rice/rubber) drive regional home renovation and construction spending.
